

![][image1]

🔬

**ANEMIA AI PRO**

*Non-Invasive Anemia Detection System*

Using Deep Learning Ensemble:

**DenseNet121  •  VGG16  •  InceptionV3**

| DenseNet121 | VGG16 | InceptionV3 | Ensemble |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **99.46%** | **100.00%** | **96.70%** | **100.00%** |
| Accuracy | Accuracy | Accuracy | Accuracy |

# **1\.  Project Overview**

AnemiaAI Pro is a non-invasive anemia detection system built using an ensemble of three state-of-the-art deep learning models. By analyzing close-up photographs of the inner lower eyelid (conjunctiva), the system classifies whether a person is Anemic or Non-Anemic — without any blood tests or laboratory visits.

Anemia affects over 2 billion people worldwide and is traditionally diagnosed through invasive blood tests. This project provides an accessible, fast, and non-invasive alternative using smartphone images of the eye conjunctiva, which appears paler in anemic individuals due to reduced hemoglobin levels.

| Property | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Project Name | AnemiaAI Pro — Anemia Detection System |
| Purpose | Non-invasive anemia detection from eye images |
| Method | Deep Learning Ensemble (3 CNN models) |
| Input | Conjunctiva (inner lower eyelid) JPEG images |
| Output | Anemic / Non-Anemic \+ Confidence percentage |
| Best Accuracy | 100.00% (VGG16 \+ Ensemble) |
| Language | Python 3.10 |
| Framework | TensorFlow 2.x / Keras |
| Platform | Windows 10/11 (Desktop App \+ CLI) |

# **2\.  Dataset**

## **2.1  Raw Dataset**

*Source: Kaggle — Palpebral Conjunctiva to Detect Anaemia*

URL: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/guptajanavi/palpebral-conjunctiva-to-detect-anaemia

| Dataset Property | Value |
| :---- | :---- |
| Total Images | 183 JPEG images |
| Anemic Class (img\_1\_\*.jpg) | 82 images (44.8%) |
| Non-Anemic Class (img\_2\_\*.jpg) | 101 images (55.2%) |
| Image Type | Conjunctiva — inner lower eyelid |
| Format | JPEG (.jpg) |
| Naming Convention | img\_1\_ \= Anemic, img\_2\_ \= Non-Anemic |

## **2.2  Preprocessed Dataset (anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv)**

The raw images are converted into a structured CSV file using setup\_dataset.py. The CSV maps each image filepath to its binary label.

**CSV Column Structure:**

  filepath,label

  images/img\_1\_1.jpg,Anemic

  images/img\_2\_7.jpg,Non-Anemic

## **2.3  Preprocessing Pipeline**

* Images resized: 224×224 for VGG16 and DenseNet121; 299×299 for InceptionV3

* Model-specific normalization applied (ImageNet mean subtraction)

* Data augmentation: rotation ±30°, width shift 20%, shear 20%, zoom 20%, horizontal flip

* Class imbalance handled: minority class upsampled to match majority class

* 5-Fold Stratified Cross Validation used to evaluate each model robustly

* Validation split: 10% of training fold used for per-epoch validation

# **3\.  Model Architecture**

The system uses a Transfer Learning approach — pre-trained ImageNet weights are used as the starting point, then fine-tuned on the anemia dataset. Three separate models are trained and their predictions are combined using an ensemble average.

## **3.1  DenseNet121**

* Base: DenseNet121 pretrained on ImageNet (121 layers with dense connections)

* Custom head: GlobalAveragePooling2D → Dropout(0.3) → Dense(1, sigmoid)

* Phase 1: Train head only (base frozen), Adam optimizer, 30 epochs

* Phase 2: Fine-tune last 30 layers, Adam(lr=1e-5), 15 epochs

* Result: 99.46% accuracy, MSE: 0.0221

## **3.2  InceptionV3**

* Base: InceptionV3 pretrained on ImageNet (input: 299×299)

* Custom head: GlobalAveragePooling2D → Dropout(0.3) → Dense(1, sigmoid)

* Phase 1: Train head only (base frozen), Adam optimizer, 30 epochs

* Phase 2: Fine-tune layers 249+, Adam(lr=1e-5), 15 epochs

* Result: 96.70% accuracy, MSE: 0.0307

## **3.3  VGG16**

* Base: VGG16 pretrained on ImageNet (16 layers)

* Custom head: GlobalAveragePooling2D → Dropout(0.3) → Dense(1, sigmoid)

* Phase 1: Train head only (base frozen), Adam optimizer, 30 epochs

* Phase 2: Fine-tune block5 conv layers, Adam(lr=1e-5), 15 epochs

* Result: 100.00% accuracy, MSE: 0.0000

## **3.4  Ensemble Method**

All three model predictions (probabilities) are averaged together:

  ensemble\_prob \= (prob\_densenet \+ prob\_vgg16 \+ prob\_inception) / 3.0

  prediction \= 'Anemic' if ensemble\_prob \> 0.5 else 'Non-Anemic'

# **4\.  Results**

| Model | Accuracy | Precision | Recall | MSE |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| DenseNet121 | 99.46% | 1.00 | 0.99 | 0.0221 |
| InceptionV3 | 96.70% | 0.97 | 0.96 | 0.0307 |
| VGG16 | 100.00% | 1.00 | 1.00 | 0.0000 |
| **Ensemble (All 3\)** | **100.00%** | **1.00** | **1.00** | **0.0127** |

*All models achieved above 90% accuracy. The ensemble of all 3 models reached 100% accuracy on the test set. Cross-validation (5-fold) was used to ensure results are robust and not overfitted.*

# **5\.  Project Folder Structure**

  anemia\_detection1/

  ├── images/                        ← 183 raw conjunctiva images

  │   ├── img\_1\_0.jpg  (Anemic)

  │   ├── img\_2\_7.jpg  (Non-Anemic)

  │   └── ...

  ├── models/                        ← saved trained model files

  │   ├── best\_densenet\_fold\_1.keras

  │   ├── best\_vgg16\_fold\_1.keras

  │   ├── best\_inception\_fold\_1.keras

  │   ├── confusion\_matrix\_densenet.png

  │   ├── confusion\_matrix\_inceptionv3.png

  │   ├── confusion\_matrix\_vgg16.png

  │   ├── confusion\_matrix\_final\_ensemble.png

  │   ├── learning\_curves\_densenet.png

  │   ├── learning\_curves\_inceptionv3.png

  │   └── learning\_curves\_vgg16.png

  ├── anemia\_env/                    ← Python virtual environment

  ├── anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv     ← preprocessed dataset

  ├── setup\_dataset.py               ← generates CSV from images

  ├── DenseNet.py                    ← DenseNet121 training script

  ├── Inception.py                   ← InceptionV3 training script

  ├── VGG.py                         ← VGG16 training script

  ├── ensembleAll.py                 ← 3-model ensemble evaluation

  ├── ensembleIncepDen.py            ← 2-model ensemble evaluation

  ├── predict.py                     ← single image prediction (CLI)

  ├── anemia\_app.py                  ← basic desktop UI

  ├── anemia\_app\_pro.py              ← advanced desktop UI (Pro)

  ├── run\_anemia\_detection.bat       ← one-click launcher (Windows)

  └── README.docx                    ← this file

# **6\.  Installation & Setup**

## **6.1  Requirements**

| Requirement | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| Operating System | Windows 10 or Windows 11 |
| Python Version | Python 3.10.x (recommended) |
| RAM | Minimum 4GB (8GB recommended) |
| Storage | Minimum 3GB free space |
| GPU | Optional (CPU works, but slower) |

## **6.2  Step-by-Step Installation**

1. Open Command Prompt (CMD) — press Windows+R, type cmd, press Enter

2. Navigate to your project folder:

    cd C:\\Users\\princ\\anemia\_detection1

3. Create virtual environment:

    python \-m venv anemia\_env

4. Activate virtual environment:

    anemia\_env\\Scripts\\activate

5. Install all required libraries:

    pip install tensorflow scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn pillow

6. Generate the dataset CSV:

    python setup\_dataset.py

# **7\.  How to Run**

## **Option A — One-Click Launcher (Easiest)**

1. Double-click run\_anemia\_detection.bat in your project folder  
2. A black window opens — type your image path and press Enter  
3. See result instantly

## **Option B — Pro Desktop App (Recommended)**

    python anemia\_app\_pro.py

Features: image preview, confidence bar, history table, session stats, live log, terminal backend

## **Option C — Command Line Prediction**

    python predict.py \--image "C:\\path\\to\\eye\_image.jpg"

## **Option D — Retrain All Models**

    python DenseNet.py \--data-csv anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv \--output-dir ./models

    python Inception.py \--data-csv anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv \--output-dir ./models

    python VGG.py \--data-csv anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv \--output-dir ./models

## **Option E — Run Ensemble Evaluation**

    python ensembleAll.py \--data-csv anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv \\

      \--densenet-model ./models/best\_densenet\_fold\_1.keras \\

      \--vgg-model ./models/best\_vgg16\_fold\_1.keras \\

      \--inception-model ./models/best\_inception\_fold\_1.keras \\

      \--output-dir ./models

# **8\.  How the System Works**

| Step | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| 1 | User provides a conjunctiva eye image (inner lower eyelid photo) |
| 2 | Image is preprocessed separately for each model (resize \+ normalize) |
| 3 | DenseNet121 analyzes image at 224×224 resolution |
| 4 | VGG16 analyzes image at 224×224 resolution |
| 5 | InceptionV3 analyzes image at 299×299 resolution |
| 6 | All 3 probability scores are averaged (ensemble) |
| 7 | If avg probability \> 0.5 → Anemic, else → Non-Anemic |
| 8 | Confidence score and risk level are displayed to user |

# **9\.  Tips for Best Results**

| Icon | Tip |
| :---- | :---- |
| ✋  Step 1 | Gently pull your lower eyelid downward with a clean finger |
| 👁  Step 2 | Expose the pink/red inner conjunctiva tissue clearly |
| 💡  Step 3 | Use bright natural light or sit near a lamp |
| 📷  Step 4 | Take a close-up photo — only the inner eyelid in frame |
| ✅  Result | 90%+ confidence \= reliable result; below 70% \= retake photo |
| 🏥  Always | Confirm any result with a qualified medical professional |

# **10\.  Troubleshooting**

| Error / Problem | Solution |
| :---- | :---- |
| anemia\_env cannot be activated | Run: Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned \-Scope CurrentUser  then use CMD not PowerShell |
| ModuleNotFoundError: tensorflow | Run: pip install tensorflow inside activated anemia\_env |
| FileNotFoundError: model .keras | You need to train models first by running DenseNet.py, VGG.py, Inception.py |
| Low confidence (below 70%) | Retake photo — pull eyelid down more, use better lighting, get closer |
| Out of memory error | Reduce batch\_size from 32 to 16 in the training scripts |
| GPU warning (TensorFlow) | This is normal on Windows — CPU training still works fine |

# **11\.  File Reference**

| File | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- |
| setup\_dataset.py | Run once — scans images folder, creates anemia\_dataset\_cleaned.csv |
| DenseNet.py | Trains DenseNet121 with 5-fold cross validation |
| Inception.py | Trains InceptionV3 with 5-fold cross validation |
| VGG.py | Trains VGG16 with 5-fold cross validation |
| ensembleAll.py | Evaluates ensemble of all 3 trained models on test set |
| ensembleIncepDen.py | Evaluates ensemble of DenseNet \+ InceptionV3 only |
| predict.py | CLI tool — predicts single image from command line |
| anemia\_app.py | Basic desktop GUI application |
| anemia\_app\_pro.py | Advanced Pro desktop GUI with history, stats, live log |
| run\_anemia\_detection.bat | One-click Windows launcher — no coding needed |
